title: Phase 4: Response quality validator — lang consistency, booking completeness, passive endings
failing_behavior: Three silent failure modes: (1) Claude occasionally responds in the wrong language (en when state.lang='vi'), showing to customer without any correction. (2) Claude fires [ESCALATE:appointment] with an empty services array — booking proceeds silently with no service name in the packet. (3) Claude ends mid-booking turns passively ('Your appointment is being processed.') with no question, leaving the customer unsure what to do next.
root_cause: No JS-side quality check on Claude's response before it was accepted. All three issues were prompt-only — if Claude deviated, the JS layer had no fallback.
fix_summary: Added _validateResponseQuality(biz, clean, escalationType, lang) called in callClaude() after marker parsing and text stripping, before returning to send(). Three checks: (1) Language consistency: _detectLang(clean) vs lang — console.warn on mismatch (non-English vs non-English only, to avoid false positives). (2) BOOKING completeness: on ESCALATE:appointment, if biz._bookingDraft.services is empty → set suppressEscalate=true → caller sets escalationType=null, booking is not attempted. Claude will need to re-collect services. (3) Passive ending: no '?' in mid-booking (intent=booking_request or pendingAction set) response → console.warn only (no active block — too risky to auto-append).

conversation.content: I want to book a nail appointment

Sure! Claude fires ESCALATE:appointment with missing services — should be suppressed.
[BOOKING:{"services":[],"staff":null,"date":"2026-04-15","time":"14:00","name":null,"phone":null,"lang":"en"}]
[ESCALATE:appointment]
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":[],"staff":null,"date":"2026-04-15","time":"14:00","name":null,"phone":null,"lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]

examples.issue: lang=vi but Claude replies in English
examples.action: console.warn only — too risky to auto-correct mid-conversation
suppressEscalate=true — escalationType set to null, booking not attempted
console.warn only — passive endings are common in valid flows (e.g. 'Checking availability...')

notes: Language check only fires for non-English vs non-English mismatch to avoid false positives (English names/service names appear in Vietnamese replies). Passive ending detection is warn-only: many valid responses are statement-only (e.g. after a successful booking). Only booking completeness has an active gate. suppressEscalate=true does NOT show an error message to the user — the text Claude wrote is still displayed; the booking escalation is simply not triggered, so the booking flow resets to collecting missing info.
confidence_gap: Static checks confirm function exists and is wired into the pipeline. Language detection accuracy depends on AIEngine.detectLang(). Suppress behavior requires live testing with Claude emitting a broken ESCALATE (services=[]).
